intro: Eulalia velutina, also known as velvet grass, is a type of grass belonging to the Poaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The bioactive constituents of Eulalia velutina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Eulalia velutina is often prepared as a tea or infused in hot water to treat respiratory issues, and it is also used in some herbal cough drops and lozenges to soothe the throat.
